overrides.comment: base.css owns the @font-face blocks, which must name real families. It is the ONE file allowed to write a literal font-family; the --font-sans/--font-mono tokens it defines are what everything else consumes.

declaration-property-value-disallowed-li.message: Banned token. --o2-* is legacy; --color-text-primary/-caption were retired (Tier 1) — use --color-text-heading / --color-text-secondary.

color-no-hex.message: Hardcoded hex is banned in .vue styles. Use a --color-* token (§3.G).

selector-disallowed-list.message: Dark mode CSS uses .dark only (Tailwind dark: variant or :where(.dark) &). §3.R

declaration-property-value-disallowed-li.message: Banned value. --o2-* is legacy; --color-text-primary/-caption were retired (Tier 1) — use --color-text-heading / --color-text-secondary. font-family must be var(--font-sans) or var(--font-mono) — no literal font stacks (FONT_AUDIT.md).
